title: Acwa wins top awards at 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid
content:
Acwa has won Desalination Company of the Year and Desalination Plant of the Year at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid, the company said on June 3.

The Saudi Arabian water and power producer manages 9.7 million cubic metres per day of desalination capacity, making it the world's largest private water desalination company. The Global Water Awards, organized by Global Water Intelligence since 2006, recognize excellence across 15 categories in the international water sector.

Dr. Tariq Nada, Head of Technical Services for Global Water at Acwa, said the awards reflected work by engineers, operators and partners across the company's ecosystem. "We are proud to provide a significant share of Saudi Arabia's desalinated water capacity, and proud that the industry has recognized the role desalination plays in strengthening water security, enabling economic growth and supporting the communities that depend on every cubic meter we deliver across the markets we serve," Nada said.

Yasir Mahmoud, Executive Vice President Global Water at Acwa, said the dual recognition signaled the company's operational model was working. "Being named Desalination Company of the Year, alongside Desalination Plant of the Year, is the clearest signal yet that our model works, lower energy intensity, higher availability and smarter financing," he said.

Acwa's Rabigh 3 Independent Water Project, a 600,000 cubic metre per day seawater reverse osmosis plant serving the Makkah region, has achieved availability levels above 99%, the company said. The facility recently completed a refinancing aligned with International Finance Corporation Blue Finance Guidelines.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-recognized-as-a-global-leader-in-water-at-the-2026-global-water-awards-in-madrid/

title: Acwa Power to build 500-MW solar-storage project in Philippines
content:
Acwa Power Philippines signed a contract to develop a 500-megawatt solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage facility on a 500-hectare site in New Clark City, the Philippines' flagship smart city development.

The project represents an initial investment of at least USD 400,000 per megawatt, with capacity of up to 500 MW upon full build-out, subject to final design and regulatory approvals. The facility will power data centers and advanced manufacturing operations within the special economic zone.

Acwa Power, a Saudi Arabia-based company and one of the world's largest private water desalination operators, signed the lease agreement with the Bases Conversion and Development Authority (BCDA), which oversees New Clark City development north of Manila.

Finance Secretary Frederick D. Go said the project supports the Philippines' sustainable economic growth and energy security objectives. "Acwa's investment in New Clark City lays the foundation upon which semiconductors, advanced manufacturing, and AI-driven industries can be built at utility scale," Go said in a statement.

The project is part of investment commitments secured by the Philippine government under President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr.'s administration.

Acwa Chief Executive Officer Dr. Samir J Serhan said the partnership reflects growing investor confidence in the Philippines for large-scale clean infrastructure. "We see an opportunity to help power industrial growth while lowering energy costs and supporting the country's broader economic ambitions through reliable renewable energy," Serhan said.

Salman M. Baray, Acwa's country general manager for the Philippines, said the project footprint allows for integrated rather than incremental development within the active special economic zone.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-bcda-advance-philippines-clean-energy-transition-with-500-mw-solar-plus-storage-project-in-new-clark-city/

title: Acwa, Emerging Power to develop up to 5,000 MW in Philippines by 2030
content:
Acwa Power Philippines and Emerging Power Inc. signed a joint development agreement to pursue up to 5,000 MW of renewable energy and gas-fired generation capacity across the Philippines, the companies said on June 2.

The partnership between Acwa, a Saudi-based power and desalination company, and EPI, the clean energy arm of nickel producer Nickel Asia Corporation, will initially focus on jointly bidding into the Philippines' Green Energy Auction Program, the government's initiative to attract renewable energy investments.

Acwa Chief Executive Officer Samir J Serhan said the Philippines has set "one of the most ambitious clean energy trajectories in Southeast Asia" and the partnership would help achieve those goals. "NAC brings deep local knowledge, an operating renewables base through EPI. We bring three decades of delivering large-scale power and water assets on time and on budget," he said in a statement.

Martin Antonio G. Zamora, Chairman and CEO of EPI, said the collaboration would "actively accelerate the nation's transition to a low-carbon economy" while building "scalable, reliable, and sustainable power infrastructure."

Salman M. Baray, Acwa's Country General Manager in the Philippines, said the agreement combines "local execution capability matched with global development muscle." He added that while the green energy auction represents an initial opportunity, the partnership aims to develop a longer pipeline of projects, including firm capacity to meet grid needs.

The agreement comes as the Philippine power sector faces rapid electrification demands and increased industrial energy consumption.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-and-emerging-power-inc-sign-joint-development-agreement-jda-to-build-up-to-5-000-mw-clean-energy-platform-by-2030-in-the-philippines/

title: EDF and Masdar Sign 15-Year Power Deal for California Solar-Storage Plant
content:
EDF power solutions North America and Masdar have signed 15-year power purchase agreements for the BigBeau Solar+Storage Project in Kern County, California, which began supplying electricity to Southern California Edison on Feb. 1, 2026.

The facility comprises a 128 megawatt solar plant and a 40 MW/160 MWh battery energy storage system. The project has been operational since December 2022 and is co-owned by both companies.

Dustin Priemer, Director Asset Management Masdar Americas, said the agreement reflects Masdar's focus on scaling utility-scale clean power in the United States. "We are appreciative of our growing partnership with Southern California Edison and our shared commitment to investing in new generation capacity to meet growing energy demand in California," he said.

Jacqueline de Fresart, Associate Director of Origination and Power Marketing at EDF power solutions North America, said the company is pleased to support SCE's clean energy goals. "We are excited to partner with SCE again and look forward to more opportunities together," she said.

BigBeau is one of seven projects that Masdar and EDF power solutions agreed to develop together, with combined capacity of 1.1 gigawatts. EDF power solutions has developed 26 gigawatts of wind, solar and storage projects over more than 35 years. Masdar, active in the United States since 2019, has invested several billion dollars in the country and plans to develop a portfolio of up to 25 GW within the next decade.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/edf-power-solutions-north-america-and-masdar-sign-agreements-for-solar-and-energy-storage-project

title: RWE, Masdar win UK approval for 3GW Dogger Bank South wind farms
content:
RWE and Masdar have received planning permission for two offshore wind farms with a combined capacity of 3 gigawatts off the northeast coast of England. Lord Whitehead, acting on behalf of the UK Energy Secretary, approved the Development Consent Order for Dogger Bank South East and West on May 14, 2026.

The projects, located over 100km off the Yorkshire and Humber coast, are being developed as a joint venture between RWE (51%) and Masdar (49%). The two 1.5GW wind farms are expected to generate enough electricity to power approximately three million UK homes annually.

Both projects secured Contracts for Difference in the UK Government's Allocation Round 7 in January 2026. The Development Consent Order application was submitted in June 2024 and included over 1,000 documents reviewed by the Planning Inspectorate, with 10 online examination hearings held.

RWE and Masdar said they will now conduct detailed final design work and pursue procurement decisions with the goal of reaching a final investment decision in 2027. The companies said the projects would create significant opportunities for businesses and communities in Yorkshire, the Humber, and wider UK.

The consent follows months of regulatory scrutiny since the application was filed. The wind farms represent a continued expansion of renewable capacity for both energy companies as the UK pursues its clean energy transition targets.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/rwe-and-masdar-receive-planning-permission-for-3gw-dogger-bank-south-offshore-wind-farm-projects

title: RWE secures £91.20/MWh contracts for 6.9GW UK offshore wind
content:
The UK government awarded RWE Contracts for Difference for 6.9 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity at a strike price of £91.20 per megawatt hour in the latest renewables auction, the company said on 14 January.

RWE's Norfolk Vanguard East, Norfolk Vanguard West, two Dogger Bank South projects and Awel y Môr secured 20-year contracts in Allocation Round 7, with prices in 2024 levels and indexed for inflation. All five projects are located in British or Irish waters.

RWE and global investment firm KKR will jointly develop, construct and operate the Norfolk Vanguard East and Norfolk Vanguard West projects. KKR will acquire a 50% equity stake in each project, combining the firm's infrastructure investment experience with RWE's offshore wind expertise. The transaction is expected to close in summer 2026, subject to customary approvals.

Markus Krebber, chief executive of RWE AG, said the company was "delighted" to secure long-term contracts for five projects. "By combining KKR's investment know-how in large-scale, complex infrastructure projects with RWE's extensive offshore wind expertise, we are well positioned to jointly realise these major projects," he said.

RWE is also partnering with Masdar on the Dogger Bank South projects and with Stadtwerke München and Siemens on Awel y Môr. The award marks a key milestone in RWE's UK offshore wind development strategy.

Source: https://uk.rwe.com/press-and-news/2026-01-14-rwe-secures-contracts-for-difference-for-6-9-gigawatts-of-offshore-wind-capacity/

title: EWEC and Masdar ink deal to deploy 30GW solar and 8GW battery storage
content:
Abu Dhabi's Emirates Water and Electricity Company signed a strategic collaboration framework with Masdar to accelerate deployment of more than 30GW of solar PV capacity and over 8GW of battery energy storage across the UAE. The agreement establishes a roadmap for utility-scale renewable energy projects designed to diversify the emirate's energy mix, strengthen supply security and support local industrial growth.

EWEC Chief Executive Ahmed Ali Alshamsi and Masdar Chief Executive Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi signed the Collaboration Framework Agreement. The deal will streamline Masdar's participation in EWEC's engagement process for renewable energy projects, from early-stage development through financial close while preserving competitive procurement transparency.

The agreement builds on prior partnerships between the companies on projects including Al Dhafra Solar PV, Al Ajban Solar PV, Khazna Solar PV and a gigascale solar and battery storage facility. EWEC aims to meet 60% of Abu Dhabi's total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035.

"This Collaboration Framework Agreement with Masdar is a pivotal strategic tool that empowers us to accelerate this transformation and meet 60 per cent of Abu Dhabi's total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035," Alshamsi said in a statement.

The framework prioritizes In-Country Value creation and development of Emirati talent and expertise. EWEC supplies integrated planning, purchasing, supply and system despatch services for water and electricity across the UAE.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/wec-and-masdar-forge-strategic-framework-to-scale-renewable-energy

title: Masdar marks 20 years as clean energy developer
content:
Abu Dhabi's Masdar is marking two decades of operations as a renewable energy developer and investor globally.

The company, established in 2006, has positioned itself as a major player in the energy transition across multiple regions and technology platforms. Masdar operates across solar, wind, hydrogen and other clean energy sectors, with investments and projects spanning continents.

The 20-year milestone reflects the company's expansion from its origins in the United Arab Emirates into an international renewable energy enterprise. The company has developed utility-scale projects and pursued strategic investments in emerging clean technologies.

Masdar's portfolio encompasses both operational assets and development pipelines in markets including Europe, Asia, Africa and the Middle East. The company has also invested in hydrogen production and other energy transition infrastructure as part of its diversification strategy.

The company's growth trajectory aligns with broader shifts in global energy markets toward decarbonization and renewable power expansion. Major energy companies and sovereign wealth funds have increasingly committed capital to clean energy development over the past two decades.

Masdar continues to compete in a crowded renewable energy sector where established players and new entrants are scaling operations to meet rising demand for wind, solar and battery storage capacity.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-20-years-of-powering-the-worlds-clean-energy-future

title: Masdar, EPCG form 50/50 joint venture for Balkans clean energy
content:
Abu Dhabi's Masdar and Montenegro's state power utility EPCG have agreed to establish a 50/50 joint venture to develop large-scale renewable energy projects across Montenegro, the Western Balkans and Southern Europe.

The partnership was signed at the Adria Future Summit in the presence of Montenegro's Energy and Mining Minister Admir Šahmanović and the UAE Ambassador to Montenegro. Masdar CEO Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi and EPCG President Milutin Đukanović signed the agreement.

The joint venture, headquartered in Nikšić, will deploy and operate clean energy projects including solar photovoltaic, wind, hydropower, pumped hydro energy storage, stand-alone battery energy storage systems and hybrid solutions across Montenegro.

The collaboration aims to support Montenegro's domestic energy needs while enabling renewable electricity exports to the Western Balkans and Southern Europe, leveraging an existing sub-sea interconnection with Italy.

Montenegro's Energy and Mining Minister Šahmanović said in a statement that the partnership "represents a turning point for the energy future of Montenegro" by combining "global knowledge, strong national capacity and a shared ambition to speed up the transition towards clean energy."

The joint venture combines EPCG's position as Montenegro's leading energy producer with Masdar's global expertise in renewable energy development. The deal builds on Masdar's existing presence in Montenegro and supports its broader expansion across the region.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-and-epcg-to-form-joint-venture

title: ScottishPower, Masdar install record UK offshore wind turbine
content:
ScottishPower and Masdar have installed the first turbine at their East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm with 115-metre blades, setting a UK record for the largest blades manufactured and deployed in the country.

The 1.4 GW project off the Suffolk coast features 95 turbines rated at 14MW each, standing approximately 262 metres tall with a rotor diameter of 236 metres. All 285 blades for the windfarm are manufactured at Siemens Gamesa's factory in Hull.

When complete, East Anglia THREE will generate enough electricity to supply more than 1.3 million homes. A single turbine revolution will produce sufficient power for a UK household for more than four days.

Charlie Jordan, ScottishPower Renewables CEO, said the project represents "a defining moment and a UK industry first" and will deliver billions of pounds in investment across UK and global supply chains while creating thousands of construction jobs and more than 100 long-term roles in East England.

The £4 billion project is being developed by ScottishPower, Iberdrola and Masdar. The partnership expects the windfarm to play a crucial role in the UK's clean energy transition and grid security.

Husain Al Meer, Director of Global Offshore Wind at Masdar, called the installation "a milestone moment for the UK's offshore wind sector."

East Anglia THREE is expected to become one of the world's largest offshore windfarms upon completion.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/first-turbine-installed-at-east-anglia-three

title: WiSER platform trains 150+ women as sustainability leaders
content:
WiSER, a global platform established in 2015 by Masdar and the Zayed Sustainability Prize, has trained more than 150 young women from 30+ nationalities to lead sustainable change through its flagship Pioneers Program.

The year-long initiative targets women aged 25-35, offering educational workshops, mentoring and networking opportunities with industry experts. Participants gain knowledge and skills to drive transition toward net-zero futures. The program has delivered over 540 hours of workshops and 565+ hours of mentoring to young women pursuing sustainability leadership roles.

WiSER operates through three pillars: Education, Engagement and Empowerment. Beyond the Pioneers Program, the platform runs WiSER Cares, a cultural exchange program combining hands-on learning across leadership, innovation, energy nexus, community engagement and global networks. A separate mentorship program pairs Pioneers with senior industry mentors to support career advancement.

The platform has convened over 4,000 influential figures to advance gender parity and sustainability. WiSER is rooted in recognition that women, often disproportionately affected by environmental challenges, can become powerful agents of change, aligned with the United Arab Emirates' sustainability vision and the legacy of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan.

WiSER will host its Annual Forum in January 2025, themed "Igniting a Global Sustainable Economy," bringing together participants and stakeholders to advance women's leadership in the energy transition.

Source: https://wiser.ae/

title: TotalEnergies, Masdar form $2.2bn joint venture for Asian renewables
content:
TotalEnergies and Masdar have signed a binding agreement to establish a $2.2 billion 50/50 joint venture that will merge their onshore renewable activities across nine Asian countries.

The joint venture will operate in Azerbaijan, Indonesia, Japan, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea and Uzbekistan, serving as the sole vehicle for developing, building, owning and operating onshore solar, wind and battery storage projects for both partners.

Each company will contribute assets of comparable value to the venture. The combined portfolio will comprise 3 GW of operational capacity and 6 GW of assets under advanced development expected to become operational by 2030.

The partnership aims to capitalize on accelerating electricity demand growth across Asia. Masdar Chairman and UAE Minister of Industry and Advanced Technology Dr Sultan Al Jaber said the collaboration would allow the partners to "accelerate our progress across the continent, unlocking new opportunities to deliver the competitive, reliable energy solutions that our partners and customers need."

TotalEnergies has established renewable energy operations across Central Asia and the Caucasus region, while Masdar operates renewable projects throughout Asia-Pacific. The company said Asia would be the main driver of global electricity demand growth this decade.

The transaction is expected to close subject to customary conditions and regulatory approvals. Financial terms indicate each company will contribute approximately $1.1 billion in assets to the venture.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/totalenergies-and-masdar

title: Masdar Drives Central Asia's Largest Renewable Push in Uzbekistan
content:
Masdar has invested more than US$2 billion in Uzbekistan's renewable energy transition since 2018, according to the latest installment of the company's Power to Change documentary series released on November 13, 2025.

The film documents Masdar's work across major solar and wind projects in the Central Asian nation, including the 500MW Zarafshan Wind Farm, the 457MW Sherabad solar project, and the 100MW Nur Navoi solar facility. These developments represent what Masdar describes as one of Central Asia's most ambitious renewable energy programs.

Ilholm Jon Teshabaev, General Director of Uzbekistan Masdar, said in the documentary, "Our relationship with power is a very old one. We know our history, our old industries and how we generated power. But the world is moving forward and so have we."

The initiatives are expanding beyond solar and wind generation. Masdar and the Government of Uzbekistan are now advancing the country's first standalone Battery Energy Storage System, which aims to balance the grid and stabilize power supply.

Abdulla Zayed, Director of Development and Investment at Masdar, stated that "from the beginning, our mission was to identify how Masdar could best support Uzbekistan's transition to a green and more sustainable energy system."

The documentary reflects broader trends of international cooperation in renewable energy development across Central Asia, highlighting how partnerships between foreign investors and governments are accelerating the shift away from traditional power sources toward cleaner alternatives.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={AF1B6ECC-3E90-4CAF-A036-6C76456D4A95}

title: Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week 2026 closes with record 60,000 attendees
content:
Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week 2026 concluded with over 60,000 attendees, marking a 20% increase from the previous year and establishing the event as the world's largest sustainability gathering.

The five-day event, held under the theme "The Nexus of Next: All Systems Go" from January 11-15, drew 15 heads of state and over 400 ministers and government officials, more than double the number from 2025. The gathering convened global leaders in energy, finance, food, water and nature to address interconnected sustainability challenges and systems transformation.

Hosted by Masdar, the clean energy company based in Abu Dhabi, the 2026 edition featured more than 240 dedicated sessions with over 800 speakers. Topics included global energy systems transformation, food and water security, and nature conservation.

The event operated under the patronage of His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the United Arab Emirates.

The expanded attendance and increased number of government officials underscored growing political momentum around sustainability commitments. The week reinforced Abu Dhabi's position as a center for clean energy leadership and sustainable development discussions.

ADSW, which includes the Zayed Sustainability Prize recognizing innovation in renewable energy and sustainability, has become a key platform for announcing climate and energy commitments by governments and private sector actors.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom

title: Standard Bank to finance Africa's renewable energy boom
content: In 2025, Standard Bank reported that renewable energy financing in Africa outpaced non-renewable energy by an 8:1 ratio, with the bank mobilizing R47.1bn in green finance during that financial year. The institution is supporting major renewable projects across the continent, including the 506MW Khauta South and West Solar projects in South Africa, the 465MW Ummbila Emoyeni wind portfolio, and the 400MW Overberg Wind Farm, which collectively represent significant capacity additions. Standard Bank has committed to mobilizing R100bn in green finance by 2028 and has already achieved 62% of its R450bn sustainable finance target, reflecting strong momentum in Africa's energy transition driven by declining technology costs, policy support, and the need to address the continent's energy access deficit affecting nearly 600 million people. This shift is critical for Africa's socio-economic development, balancing decarbonization,

title: MENA emerges as one of the world’s largest clean energy pipelines as global clean industry financing doubles in pace
content: The Middle East and North Africa region is experiencing rapid growth in clean industrial financing, with 84 announced projects representing $642 billion in potential investment across clean fuels, fertilizers, steel, and aluminum production. Egypt leads the region by project count with 25 projects worth $108.5 billion, while Oman has advanced furthest with a 19-project pipeline valued at $271 billion and a green ammonia facility already at final investment decision. Saudi Arabia's NEOM Green Hydrogen Project, a 1.2 million-tonne-per-year clean ammonia facility led by NEOM, Air Products, and ACWA Power, represents the region's most significant milestone and has attracted international capital and technology. Clean fuels for aviation and shipping are emerging as the region's strongest near-term opportunity, with strategic positioning near major aviation hubs and proximity to export markets making MENA increasingly competitive in global decarbonized

title: Morocco to invest $15bln in renewable energy over 5 years
content: Morocco plans to invest $16 billion over the next five years to add approximately 16 GW of renewable energy capacity to its electrical grid, according to Morocco's Minister of Energy Transition Leila Benali. The North African country has already achieved renewable energy sources comprising more than 45 percent of its energy mix, representing a cumulative capacity of over 45 GW built through substantial investments over recent decades. Beyond expanding solar and renewable generation, Morocco is implementing self-consumption and microgrid reforms that allow citizens to install solar panels and feed excess electricity back into the national grid, democratizing energy production. This significant renewable energy expansion positions Morocco as a regional leader in clean energy adoption and addresses growing electricity demand while advancing climate and sustainability goals in the Middle East and North Africa region.

title: UAE's Alcazar signs agreements for Egypt's 580MW Gabal El-Zeit wind farm
content: UAE-based renewable energy investor Alcazar Energy Partners (AEP) has signed definitive agreements to acquire Egypt's 580-megawatt Gabal El-Zeit wind farm in the Red Sea region through a $420 million investment financed externally. The Dubai company will undertake investment, operations, and management of the asset via a Power Purchase Agreement with the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Company and agreements with the New and Renewable Energy Authority under a 25-year typical term structure. This transaction represents a key milestone in Egypt's privatization program and aligns with the country's target of achieving 45 percent renewable energy in its power generation mix by 2028. Combined with AEP's concurrent 500-megawatt NIAT Wind project negotiations, these deals are expected to mobilize close to $1 billion for Egypt's clean energy sector, establishing a benchmark model for future utility-scale public-private partnerships in the region.

title: Energy storage boom fuels Oman’s renewable future
content: Energy storage is rapidly emerging as a critical component of the global energy transition, with battery systems becoming essential for managing the intermittent nature of renewable energy. Chinese battery manufacturer CATL projects that energy storage could represent half of its global sales by 2030, while Brazil is preparing a major auction to procure at least 2 GW of storage capacity, and developers globally are increasingly combining solar, wind, and battery storage projects with green hydrogen production. For Oman specifically, energy storage represents a significant opportunity to support its growing renewable energy ambitions in solar and wind development, particularly in areas like Duqm and Al Wusta, by improving grid stability and enabling the country to maximize efficiency of its abundant clean energy resources and position itself as a regional exporter of clean energy products.

title: Consultant sought for Marsa LNG solar power project in Oman
content: Oman is developing the Marsa Solar IPP, a 280 MW solar power project in Al Dakhiliyah Governorate, to support the decarbonization of the Marsa LNG bunkering facility being built at SOHAR Port by a consortium led by TotalEnergies (80%) and OQ Exploration & Production (20%). Both projects are expected to be operational by the first half of 2028, with the $1.6 billion Marsa LNG facility representing the first LNG-based bunkering facility in the Middle East. The solar plant will supply 44% of the facility's daytime energy needs while exporting approximately 56% of daytime output to Oman's spot market, enabling Marsa LNG to achieve a greenhouse gas intensity of below 3 kg CO₂e/boe, significantly lower than the global average of 35 kg CO

title: ADIA joins $293mln capital raise by India’s ACME Solar
content: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) joined other Indian and global investors in ACME Solar Holdings' 28 billion Indian rupee ($292.7 million) qualified institutions placement, marking the renewable energy company's first capital raise since its 2024 stock exchange listing. The qualified institutions placement attracted strong participation from marquee investors including BlackRock and Goldman Sachs from the US, Pictet from Switzerland, and Amundi from France, alongside existing Indian investors. ACME Solar will use the proceeds to reduce leverage and strengthen its balance sheet, while its parent ACME Group continues developing Oman's first green hydrogen project in Duqm scheduled for completion by end of 2026. This investment demonstrates continued Middle Eastern and global investor confidence in Indian renewable energy companies and regional green hydrogen initiatives.

title: Arab renewable energy soars 36% in 2025
content: Arab Energy Organisation member states, led by Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Egypt, increased renewable energy production capacity by 35.9 percent in 2025, adding 10.3 GW to reach 39.2 GW total capacity. Saudi Arabia contributed the largest share with 5.7 GW, followed by Egypt (1.5 GW), UAE (1.3 GW), and Qatar (0.9 GW), with solar energy dominating at 72.3 percent of capacity, hydroelectric at 16.9 percent, and wind at 9.7 percent. While this represents substantial regional growth, the Arab states' renewable capacity remains only 0.8 percent of the global total of 5,149 GW, reflecting the region's early-stage renewable transition. These investments support Arab nations' strategy to preserve oil and gas reserves for export while advancing environmental sustainability goals.

title: Oman opens technical bids for 1GW battery storage consultancy tender
content: Oman's Authority for Public Services Regulation has opened technical bids for an advisory tender supporting a 1,000 MW / four-hour Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project, with a total storage capacity of 4,000 MWh. Thirteen international engineering and consulting firms, including Tractebel Engineering, WSP International, DNV, and Mott Macdonald, submitted technical proposals on May 25, 2026, to provide independent regulatory, technical, and commercial validation services. The selected consultant will support this large-scale energy storage scheme, which represents a significant step in Oman's renewable energy infrastructure development. This project aligns with Oman's broader renewable energy expansion, following the recent power purchase agreement signed for the country's first utility-scale, round-the-clock renewable energy project.

title: Kuwait expected to announce winning bidder for 1.1GW Shagaya solar project in Q3
content: Kuwait is proceeding with the development of a 1.1 GW solar photovoltaic project at the Shagaya Renewable Energy Park, with the winning bidder expected to be announced in the third quarter of 2026, following financial bid opening by mid-July 2026. Three consortia have submitted technical proposals: Saudi Arabia's ACWA partnered with Kuwait's Alternative Energy Projects Company, UAE's Masdar with Kuwait's Fouad Alghanim & Sons, and France's EDF Renewables with Kuwait's Abdullah Al Hamad Al Sagar and Brothers Company and South Korea's KOWEPO. Separately, Kuwait extended the bid submission deadline for a 500 MW solar project in Zone 2 to July 26, 2026, with seven consortia and companies competing for the contract. These projects represent significant progress in Kuwait's renewable energy expansion strategy within the broader Middle Eastern energy transition.

title: Mezzanine and Open Access Energy partner to accelerate virtual wheeling adoption across South Africa’s renewable energy market
content:
UNAVAILABLE

The provided text focuses entirely on South Africa's energy market and virtual wheeling infrastructure developments between Mezzanine and Open Access Energy. There is no content related to renewable energy developments, projects, or initiatives in the Middle East region. The article discusses South African electricity market liberalization, Eskom's wheeling mechanisms, and energy procurement for corporate buyers in South Africa, which falls outside the scope of Middle East renewable energy relevance.

title: Sungrow commissions 1.4 MWp solar plant to power Egypt’s desert oil facility
content: Sungrow, ACO, and Korra Energi have commissioned a 1.4 MWp solar system at the Esh El Mallaha petroleum facility in Hurghada, Egypt, replacing diesel-based power generation at the remote oil and gas operations site. The system uses nine SG150CX string inverters and is expected to generate 125 MWh annually while reducing 125 tonnes of CO2 emissions each year, addressing persistent energy challenges in Egypt's remote oil and gas sector. The project demonstrates how onsite solar integration can enhance operational stability and cost efficiency while reducing reliance on diesel in facilities constrained by weak grid access, extreme heat, and dust. This initiative is particularly relevant to the Middle East as it showcases renewable energy adoption in the oil and gas sector, a critical step toward improving energy efficiency and emissions management in a region traditionally dependent on fossil fuels.

title: Sungrow, Korra Energi commission solar project at Egypt oil facility
content: Sungrow, ACO, and Korra Energi have commissioned a 1.4 MWp solar system at Egypt's Esh El Mallaha petroleum facility in Hurghada, replacing diesel generation with clean electricity to improve operational reliability in remote oil and gas operations. The installation features nine SG150CX string inverters and is expected to generate 125 MWh annually while reducing CO2 emissions by 125 tonnes per year. The project addresses critical energy challenges in Egypt's desert operations by reducing reliance on diesel fuel, improving grid stability, and lowering operational costs in a sector hampered by weak grid access and extreme environmental conditions. This initiative demonstrates how onsite solar integration can enhance energy efficiency and resilience in the oil and gas sector while supporting Egypt's renewable energy transition.

title: China's AIKO to supply modules for Nefer Benban solar plus storage project in Egypt
content: China-based AIKO has signed a module supply agreement with Infinity Power (a joint venture between UAE's Masdar and Egypt's Infinity Energy) to supply solar panels for the Nefer Benban solar project in Aswan, Egypt. The 259 MW solar facility integrated with 120 MWh of battery storage is expected to generate 471.6 GWh of annual electricity with lifecycle revenue of $566 million, scheduled to connect to the grid in Q4 2026 at a total project cost of approximately $180 million. The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development is providing $70 million in senior debt financing for the project, which is 51% owned by Infinity Power Holding and 49% by HAU Energy. This project represents a significant renewable energy investment in Egypt, advancing the country's solar capacity expansion and energy storage capabilities in the Middle East region.

title: South Africa: Renewable energy development driving change in Nelson Mandela Bay
content: South Africa's renewable energy transition is advancing with Phase 1A of Parsons Power Park breaking ground in Nelson Mandela Bay, a 31 MWp solar project developed by Yellow Door Energy, Raw Renewables, and Natura Energy. The 58-hectare installation featuring 47,000 solar panels on single-axis trackers is expected to generate approximately 60 GWh of renewable energy annually while avoiding 54,360 tonnes of carbon emissions, coming online in Q1 2027. The wheeled solar project will supply electricity to large energy users within Nelson Mandela Bay's municipal network through PowerX, the first private sector electricity trader licensed by South Africa's National Energy Regulator, with Alensy Energy Solutions serving as the EPC partner. This development demonstrates how large-scale commercial solar projects are enabling South African businesses to access cleaner, lower-cost renewable energy while supporting local job creation and strengthening national

title: Sungrow and Masdar sign 7.5GWh energy storage system for Abu Dhabi's World First RTC Project
content: Sungrow and Masdar have signed an agreement to supply energy storage systems and PV inverter solutions for the UAE's Round-the-Clock (RTC) renewable energy project, a world-first gigascale initiative combining 5.2GW of solar PV capacity with a 19GWh battery energy storage system. Sungrow will provide 7.5GWh of PowerTitan 3.0 energy storage systems and 2.6GW of PV inverter solutions to enable continuous baseload renewable energy delivery with 90 percent round-trip efficiency and 99.3 percent maximum efficiency. The project, expected to be operational in 2027, represents a major advancement in utility-scale renewable energy integration for the Middle East, designed to overcome intermittency challenges and serve energy-intensive industries, businesses, and emerging digital infrastructure at a globally competitive tariff while establishing a replicable blueprint for

title: Masdar signs supply deal with China's Sungrow for Abu Dhabi RTC renewable energy project
content: UAE-based Masdar has signed an agreement with China's Sungrow to supply energy storage systems and photovoltaic inverter solutions for Abu Dhabi's round-the-clock (RTC) renewable energy project. Sungrow will provide 7.5 gigawatt-hours of battery energy storage and 2.6 gigawatts of PV inverter solutions, following earlier supply agreements with JinkoSolar for 2 gigawatts of PV modules. The RTC project, jointly developed by Masdar and Emirates Water and Electricity Company, will integrate 5.2 gigawatts of solar generation capacity with 19 gigawatt-hours of battery storage to deliver baseload renewable electricity continuously starting in 2027. Once operational, the project will become the world's first gigascale renewable energy facility capable of providing 24/7 baseload power, significantly advancing the

title: 31st IRENA Council ushers new era of global energy transition
content: The International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) held its thirty-first Council meeting in Abu Dhabi with over 300 government representatives attending in hybrid format to review progress on the global energy transition. The Council discussed strengthening energy security through renewables and the role of emerging technologies including artificial intelligence, digitalization, and electrification in accelerating the renewable-based transition. IRENA Director-General Francesco La Camera emphasized the critical nature of the moment as member nations establish priorities for the next phase, while UAE Minister Dr. Amna bint Abdullah Al Dahak stressed that energy security, economic stability, and food security are interconnected, making renewable energy acceleration essential to global resilience. The 171-member agency will implement its 2026-2027 Work Programme and Budget while developing its Medium-term Strategy through 2032 to maintain its leadership in the global energy transition.

title: Global wind and solar power outpace gas for first time in April, report shows
content: Wind and solar power generated 22% of global electricity in April, surpassing gas at 20% for the first time ever, according to analysis by UK think tank Ember. Global combined wind and solar output grew 13% year-on-year, with significant increases across major markets including China (+14%), the European Union (+13%), Britain (+35%), the United States (+8%), Australia (+17%), Chile (+24%), and Brazil (+4%). The milestone reflects a broader structural shift strengthened by current energy crises, as renewables increasingly reduce reliance on gas imports and provide stronger economic advantages compared to imported fossil fuels. This trend is particularly significant for global energy security and the acceleration of renewable deployment across diverse regions.

title: Electrification to lead the next phase of energy transition
content: IRENA released a comprehensive report warning that while tripling renewable power capacity and doubling energy efficiency by 2030 remain essential goals, they are insufficient alone to achieve the global energy transition and meet the 1.5°C climate target. The agency's revised scenario projects electricity's share of global consumption rising from 23% today to 35% by 2035 and over 50% by 2050, with fossil fuels declining from 80% to 50% by 2035 and 20% or less by 2050. A critical infrastructure bottleneck exists with approximately 2,500 GW of wind and solar awaiting grid connection, requiring grid investment needs of USD 1.2 trillion annually—more than double the USD 0.5 trillion currently invested—to support electrification across transport, industry, buildings, and digital sectors. The report emphasizes that rapid electrification powered by renewables is

title: Energy storage powers Oman’s renewable drive
content: Oman is accelerating its renewable energy transition with massive solar and wind projects expected to supply nearly 10% of grid electricity by 2026, with installed renewable capacity projected to reach 8.8 GW by 2030, up from current levels. The nation's expansion includes pioneering projects like the 50 MW Dhofar wind farm and 1500 MW solar plants in Ibri and Manah, driven by commitments to energy security and net-zero targets. However, experts warn that without large-scale energy storage systems, grid stability and efficiency cannot be maintained as renewable intermittency increases, making storage integration critical once renewables exceed 20% of supply. Oman is addressing this through diverse technologies including the 500 MW Ibri III solar project with 100 MWh battery storage, green hydrogen auctions led by Hydrom, and decentralized storage initiatives, positioning the country as a leading

title: Arab Energy Fund prices $500mln 5-year sukuk as demand tops $900mln
content: The Arab Energy Fund successfully priced a $500 million five-year sukuk at a profit rate of 4.686%, with the senior unsecured trust certificates priced at par with a 70 basis point spread over SOFR and tightening from initial guidance of SOFR plus 80 bps. The issuance received strong investor demand with books exceeding $900 million, allowing the issuer to price at the tight end of guidance, and carries expected ratings of Aa2 from Moody's and AA+ from Fitch. The sukuk will mature on June 16, 2031 and be listed on Euronext Dublin and Nasdaq Dubai, with proceeds allocated for general corporate purposes, reflecting the region's continued use of Islamic finance instruments for capital raising.

title: Dubai Islamic Bank prices $1bln AT1 PNC 6-year sukuk
content: Dubai Islamic Bank, the UAE's largest Islamic bank, successfully raised $1 billion through a perpetual non-call six-year benchmark AT1 sukuk with a profit rate of 6.250%, attracting strong institutional demand of $1.85 billion from 85 accounts across Europe, Asia, and the Middle East. The issuance saw 83% of allocations going to the MENA region and 17% to international investors, demonstrating significant interest in Islamic finance instruments in the region. This follows the bank's November sustainability-linked sukuk issuance of $1 billion, highlighting the growing role of Islamic banking in financing regional development and sustainable projects in the Middle East.

title: Oman’s $4.2bln green hydrogen project’s 2nd phase to start operation in 2030
content: India's ACME Group is developing a $4.2 billion green hydrogen and ammonia project in Oman's Duqm Special Economic Zone, with China's Sungrow Hydrogen supplying 160MW of alkaline water electrolysis units. The first phase, targeting commissioning by end of 2026, will produce 100,000 tonnes of green ammonia annually, while the second and third phases (beginning operations in 2030 and 2033) will each add 400,000 tonnes of green ammonia and 71,000 tonnes of green hydrogen capacity, bringing total annual production to 800,000 tonnes of green ammonia and 142,000 tonnes of green hydrogen. The project represents a significant renewable energy investment for Oman, part of a broader investment agreement package valued at $7.5 billion, with the first phase supported by an offtake agreement with Norway's Y

title: COP31 Presidency announces 35% by 2035 electrification target as part of Action Agenda launch
content: Turkey announced ambitious climate targets at the UN June Climate Meetings in Bonn as the incoming COP31 President, including raising electricity's share of final energy demand from over 20% to 35% by 2035, reducing building sector energy consumption intensity by at least 25% by 2035, and halving global waste growth by 2035. COP31 President-Designate Murat Kurum launched a global electrification initiative calling for accelerated transition from direct fossil fuel use to clean electricity across buildings, transport, and industry. The COP31 summit will be held in Antalya, Turkey in November 2026. These targets represent significant commitments to renewable energy expansion and energy efficiency improvements relevant to decarbonization efforts in the Middle East and globally.

title: DEWA promotes clean energy strategy through flagship Al Shera'a project
content: DEWA's new headquarters Al Shera'a in Dubai, the world's tallest and smartest net-positive government building, was showcased to Masdar's CEO Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi during a visit. The 19-floor, two million square-foot facility operates with over 110,000 smart sensors, 3,200 network devices, and artificial intelligence to generate more than 1.9 million automated control commands daily, producing more clean energy than it consumes. The building achieved LEED Platinum and WELL Gold certifications and represents DEWA's commitment to Dubai's Clean Energy Strategy 2050 and Net Zero Carbon Emissions Strategy 2050 goals. This innovative net-positive building model demonstrates technological integration for sustainable operations and positions the UAE as a leader in green building standards within the Middle East region.
